title: US carries out strikes in Iran after vessel attack
gist: US warplanes struck Iranian coastal radar and missile/drone storage after Iran allegedly hit the Singapore-flagged cargo ship Ever Lovely with a drone ~7.5 nm off Oman, the first attack on Hormuz shipping since the 60-day ceasefire extension; the incident forced the UN to pause guiding 11,000+ stranded ships, with Iran asserting it cannot guarantee safety of vessels that don't coordinate transit with Tehran.

themes.theme: Hormuz shipping as the trigger point

insights.claim: Iran allegedly hit the Singapore-flagged cargo ship Ever Lovely with a one-way attack drone ~7.5 nautical miles off Oman's coast, the first reported attack on Hormuz shipping since the 60-day ceasefire extension.
insights.speaker: Jared Szuba
insights.so_what: Resumption of shipping attacks post-ceasefire is the leading indicator for a renewed Hormuz risk premium — each strike is a direct catalyst for crude and freight rates.
insights.quote: It was the first reported attack on shipping in the waterway since the United States and Iran reached an agreement to extend the ceasefire by 60 days
insights.locator: It was the first reported attack on shipping

insights.claim: The attack forced the UN's maritime agency to pause and reassess its mission to guide more than 11,000 stranded ships through Hormuz, just two days after it began.
insights.speaker: Jared Szuba
insights.so_what: 11,000+ ships stranded quantifies the scale of bottlenecked trade; the pause means the supply-normalization that pressured oil lower is reversible at a single incident.
insights.quote: The incident forced the UN's maritime agency to pause and reassess its mission to guide more than 11,000 stranded ships through Hormuz
insights.locator: The attack on the Ever Lovely damaged the ship's bridge

insights.claim: Iran's deputy FM Gharibabadi warned that ship safety in Hormuz 'cannot be guaranteed under ambiguous arrangements, parallel routes' that disregard Iran's role as the coastal state.
insights.speaker: Jared Szuba
insights.so_what: Iran is explicitly conditioning safe passage on ships coordinating with Tehran, rejecting the US/Oman southern lane — a durable structural threat to free transit.
insights.quote: cannot be guaranteed under ambiguous arrangements, parallel routes, or decision-making processes that disregard Iran's considerations as the coastal state
insights.locator: Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister for Legal and International Affairs Kazem Gharibabadi posted on X

insights.claim: US Central Command struck Iranian missile and drone storage and coastal radar at Sirik in response to the Ever Lovely attack, with the IRGC vowing to retaliate 'at a time and place of our choosing'.
insights.speaker: Jared Szuba
insights.so_what: Targeting radar and drone storage shows the US is degrading Iran's Hormuz strike capability — but IRGC retaliation pledges keep the escalation cycle open.
insights.quote: US aircraft struck Iranian missile and drone storage locations and coastal radar sites after Iran hit M/V Ever Lovely
insights.locator: American warplanes bombed targets along Iran's southern coast

insights.claim: A US official downplayed the strikes as not rising to the level of resuming the conflict, signaling Washington's intent to keep escalation contained within the ceasefire.
insights.speaker: Jared Szuba
insights.so_what: US framing of strikes as sub-war calibration suggests both sides want to preserve the deal — a dovish read that caps near-term escalation odds.
insights.quote: A US official speaking not for attribution downplayed the US strikes in Iran on Friday, saying they did not rise to the level of resuming the conflict.
insights.locator: A US official speaking not for attribution
